get.summary: Enumerates all the DID, rev, and commit CID for all repos hosted by this service. Does not require auth; implemented by PDS and Relay.

Repository event stream, aka Firehose endpoint. Outputs repo commits with diff data, and identity update events, for all repositories on the current server. See the atproto specifications for details around stream sequencing, repo versioning, CAR diff format, and more. Public and does not require auth; implemented by PDS and Relay.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The last known event seq number to backfill from.

schema.description: The last known event seq number to backfill from.

get.summary: Describes the server's account creation requirements and capabilities. Implemented by PDS.

inviteCodeRequired.description: If true, an invite code must be supplied to create an account on this instance.

phoneVerificationRequired.description: If true, a phone verification token must be supplied to create an account on this instance.

availableUserDomains.description: List of domain suffixes that can be used in account handles.

get.summary: Get information about an account and repository, including the list of collections. Does not require auth.

didDoc.description: The complete DID document for this account.

collections.description: List of all the collections (NSIDs) for which this repo contains at least one record.

handleIsCorrect.description: Indicates if handle is currently valid (resolves bi-directionally)

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The handle or DID of the repo.

schema.description: The handle or DID of the repo.

parameters.name: cid

post.summary: Upload a new blob, to be referenced from a repository record. The blob will be deleted if it is not referenced within a time window (eg, minutes). Blob restrictions (mimetype, size, etc) are enforced when the reference is created. Requires auth, implemented by PDS.
Submit a moderation report regarding an atproto account or record. Implemented by moderation services (with PDS proxying), and requires auth.

reasonType.description: Indicates the broad category of violation the report is for.

reason.description: Additional context about the content and violation.

post.summary: Request that the server re-resolve an identity (DID and handle). The server may ignore this request, or require authentication, depending on the role, implementation, and policy of the server.

get.summary: Resolves an atproto handle (hostname) to a DID. Does not necessarily bi-directionally verify against the the DID document.

parameters.name: handle
parameters.description: The handle to resolve.

schema.description: The handle to resolve.

get.summary: Get a skeleton of a feed provided by a feed generator. Auth is optional, depending on provider requirements, and provides the DID of the requester. Implemented by Feed Generator Service.

parameters.name: feed
parameters.description: Reference to feed generator record describing the specific feed being requested.

schema.description: Reference to feed generator record describing the specific feed being requested.

get.summary: Get detailed profile view of an actor. Does not require auth, but contains relevant metadata with auth.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Handle or DID of account to fetch profile of.

schema.description: Handle or DID of account to fetch profile of.

from.description: Text to search for and replace.

place.stream.server.defs_webhook.description: A webhook configuration for receiving Streamplace events.

url.description: The webhook URL where events will be sent.

events.description: The types of events this webhook should receive.

active.description: Whether this webhook is currently active.

prefix.description: Text to prepend to webhook messages.

suffix.description: Text to append to webhook messages.

rewrite.description: Text replacement rules for webhook messages.

updatedAt.description: When this webhook was last updated.

name.description: A user-friendly name for this webhook.

description.description: A description of what this webhook is used for.

lastTriggered.description: When this webhook was last triggered.

errorCount.description: Number of consecutive errors for this webhook.

muteWords.description: Words to filter out from chat messages. Messages containing any of these words will not be forwarded.

viewerCount.description: The number of viewers watching this livestream. Use when you can't reasonably use #viewerCount directly.

app.bsky.actor.defs_viewerState.description: Metadata about the requesting account's relationship with the subject account. Only has meaningful content for authed requests.

com.atproto.label.defs_label.description: Metadata tag on an atproto resource (eg, repo or record).

ver.description: The AT Protocol version of the label object.

src.description: DID of the actor who created this label.

uri.description: AT URI of the record, repository (account), or other resource that this label applies to.

cid.description: Optionally, CID specifying the specific version of 'uri' resource this label applies to.

val.description: The short string name of the value or type of this label.

neg.description: If true, this is a negation label, overwriting a previous label.

cts.description: Timestamp when this label was created.

exp.description: Timestamp at which this label expires (no longer applies).

app.bsky.actor.defs_knownFollowers.description: The subject's followers whom you also follow

app.bsky.actor.defs_verificationState.description: Represents the verification information about the user this object is attached to.

verifications.description: All verifications issued by trusted verifiers on behalf of this user. Verifications by untrusted verifiers are not included.

verifiedStatus.description: The user's status as a verified account.

trustedVerifierStatus.description: The user's status as a trusted verifier.

app.bsky.actor.defs_verificationView.description: An individual verification for an associated subject.

issuer.description: The user who issued this verification.

uri.description: The AT-URI of the verification record.

isValid.description: True if the verification passes validation, otherwise false.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the verification was created.

embed.description: An optional embed associated with the status.

expiresAt.description: The date when this status will expire. The application might choose to no longer return the status after expiration.

isActive.description: True if the status is not expired, false if it is expired. Only present if expiration was set.

status.description: If active=false, this optional field indicates a possible reason for why the account is not active. If active=false and no status is supplied, then the host makes no claim for why the repository is no longer being hosted.

com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos_commit.description: Represents an update of repository state. Note that empty commits are allowed, which include no repo data changes, but an update to rev and signature.

seq.description: The stream sequence number of this message.

tooBig.description: DEPRECATED -- replaced by #sync event and data limits. Indicates that this commit contained too many ops, or data size was too large. Consumers will need to make a separate request to get missing data.

repo.description: The repo this event comes from. Note that all other message types name this field 'did'.

rev.description: The rev of the emitted commit. Note that this information is also in the commit object included in blocks, unless this is a tooBig event.

oneOf.description: The rev of the last emitted commit from this repo (if any).

blocks.description: CAR file containing relevant blocks, as a diff since the previous repo state. The commit must be included as a block, and the commit block CID must be the first entry in the CAR header 'roots' list.

items.description: List of repo mutation operations in this commit (eg, records created, updated, or deleted).
DEPRECATED -- will soon always be empty. List of new blobs (by CID) referenced by records in this commit.

prevData.description: The root CID of the MST tree for the previous commit from this repo (indicated by the 'since' revision field in this message). Corresponds to the 'data' field in the repo commit object. NOTE: this field is effectively required for the 'inductive' version of firehose.

time.description: Timestamp of when this message was originally broadcast.

com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos_repoOp.description: A repo operation, ie a mutation of a single record.

oneOf.description: For creates and updates, the new record CID. For deletions, null.

prev.description: For updates and deletes, the previous record CID (required for inductive firehose). For creations, field should not be defined.

com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos_sync.description: Updates the repo to a new state, without necessarily including that state on the firehose. Used to recover from broken commit streams, data loss incidents, or in situations where upstream host does not know recent state of the repository.

seq.description: The stream sequence number of this message.

did.description: The account this repo event corresponds to. Must match that in the commit object.

blocks.description: CAR file containing the commit, as a block. The CAR header must include the commit block CID as the first 'root'.

rev.description: The rev of the commit. This value must match that in the commit object.

time.description: Timestamp of when this message was originally broadcast.

com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos_identity.description: Represents a change to an account's identity. Could be an updated handle, signing key, or pds hosting endpoint. Serves as a prod to all downstream services to refresh their identity cache.

handle.description: The current handle for the account, or 'handle.invalid' if validation fails. This field is optional, might have been validated or passed-through from an upstream source. Semantics and behaviors for PDS vs Relay may evolve in the future; see atproto specs for more details.

com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos_account.description: Represents a change to an account's status on a host (eg, PDS or Relay). The semantics of this event are that the status is at the host which emitted the event, not necessarily that at the currently active PDS. Eg, a Relay takedown would emit a takedown with active=false, even if the PDS is still active.

active.description: Indicates that the account has a repository which can be fetched from the host that emitted this event.

status.description: If active=false, this optional field indicates a reason for why the account is not active.

com.atproto.moderation.createReport_modT.description: Moderation tool information for tracing the source of the action

meta.description: Additional arbitrary metadata about the source

handle.description: The validated handle of the account; or 'handle.invalid' if the handle did not bi-directionally match the DID document.

didDoc.description: The complete DID document for the identity.

feedContext.description: Context that will be passed through to client and may be passed to feed generator back alongside interactions.
